projects.title: Stability of earthen dam
projects.description: 5 The proposed study aims to develop an AI-based distress detection and classification system for pavement infrastructure using advanced deep learning techniques. The system will be capable of automatically identifying different types of pavement distress, such as cracks, potholes, rutting, ravelling, and surface deformation, from field images or video data. In addition to detecting the distress, the model will localize the damaged regions and assess the severity level with high accuracy and reliability. Such an approach can significantly reduce manual inspection efforts, improve consistency in condition assessment, and enable rapid monitoring of large pavement networks.

projects.title: A Hybrid AI Approach for Smart Healthcare: Enhancing Disease Prediction and Clinical Decision Support
projects.description: 17 18 19 This work proposes a hybrid AI-based smart healthcare system that integrates multiple machine learning and deep learning techniques to enhance disease prediction and clinical decision support. The system combines data-driven models such as neural networks, ensemble learning, and probabilistic methods to analyze heterogeneous healthcare data, including patient history, diagnostic reports, and real-time sensor inputs. By leveraging hybrid intelligence, the framework improves prediction accuracy, robustness, and adaptability across diverse medical conditions. Additionally, the system incorporates a decision support module that assists clinicians by providing evidence-based recommendations and risk assessments. The proposed approach aims to reduce diagnostic errors, enable early disease detection, and support personalized treatment planning. Overall, the framework contributes to the development of intelligent, scalable, and efficient healthcare solutions aligned with modern digital health ecosystems.

projects.title: DriveSafeAI 2.0: A Multimodal Edge-AI Framework for Real-Time Driver Drowsiness Detection and Intelligent Transportation Safety
projects.description: Speech and Facial based emotion detection using VR and Machine Learning DriveSafeAI 2.0 is a multimodal edge-AI framework designed to detect driver drowsiness in real time by integrating visual, behavioral, and sensor-based data. It leverages on-device intelligence for low-latency processing, ensuring timely alerts and enhanced road safety. The system aims to contribute to intelligent transportation by reducing fatigue-related accidents through scalable and costeffective deployment. Speech-based emotion detection using machine learning is an emerging area of research that focuses on identifying human emotions from vocal signals. It involves analyzing various acoustic features such as pitch, tone, energy, and speech rate to classify emotions like happiness, sadness, anger, and neutrality. Feature extraction techniques such as Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) and Mel-spectrograms are commonly used to capture both spectral and temporal characteristics of speech signals. Machine learning and deep learning models, including Support Vector Machines (SVM), Random Forest, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), are employed to learn patterns associated with different emotional states. Data augmentation and noise reduction techniques further enhance model robustness and accuracy. This technology has wide applications in human-computer interaction, mental health monitoring, customer service, and virtual assistants, making systems more intuitive and responsive to human emotions.

projects.title: WiTrack: Tracking human activity using WiFi routers
projects.description: Traditional human activity detection systems rely heavily on cameras, wearable devices, or dedicated sensors, which introduce challenges such as privacy concerns, user inconvenience, limited scalability, and higher deployment costs. In environments like homes, hospitals, and offices, continuous monitoring using these methods is often impractical or intrusive. Recent advances show that WiFi signals inherently capture environmental changes, as human movement alters signal propagation characteristics such as Channel State Information (CSI). However, leveraging WiFi signals for reliable activity recognition presents several challenges: Extracting meaningful features from noisy and high-dimensional CSI data Ensuring robustness across different environments and users Achieving real-time performance on resource-constrained edge devices Maintaining high accuracy without extensive labeled datasets Therefore, the problem is to design and implement a low-cost, privacypreserving, device-free sensing system that uses WiFi CSI data to accurately detect and classify human activities in real time, while being robust, scalable, and deployable on commodity hardware such as ESP32 devices.

projects.title: Metasurface Absorber for Bunker Design and Stealth Applications
projects.description: This project focuses on designing a metasurface absorber tailored for bunker applications, enabling broadband electromagnetic absorption, structural stealth, and resilience against high-power microwave threats. The design leverages parametric optimization to enhance key performance metrics including absorptivity, bandwidth, angular stability, and structural integration while ensuring mechanical robustness for defense-grade deployment. Dr Rajesh Khanna

projects.title: AI-Enabled Design and Optimization of Silver/Graphene Ink–Based Printed MIMO Antenna for 5G Communication
projects.description: The rapid advancement of 5G systems has created a strong demand for compact, flexible, and high-performance antenna technologies capable of supporting high data rates and reliable multi-channel communication. In this context, Silver/Graphene ink–based printed antennas have emerged as a promising solution, offering a combination of high electrical conductivity from silver and mechanical flexibility and chemical stability from graphene. These properties make them particularly suitable for next-generation flexible and wearable wireless devices 9855014327

projects.title: Artificial Intelligence for Early Prediction and Risk Stratification of Chronic Heart Failure
projects.description: The application of Artificial Intelligence in predicting Chronic Heart Failure offers significant potential for early diagnosis and improved patient outcomes. This topic focuses on leveraging machine learning and deep learning models to analyze clinical, imaging, and wearable sensor data for accurate risk prediction and disease progression monitoring. The scope includes integrating electronic health records, ECG signals, and imaging data to develop robust predictive systems. The importance lies in reducing hospital readmissions, enabling timely interventions, and supporting personalized treatment strategies. Incorporating explainability ensures that predictions are transparent and clinically interpretable, increasing trust among healthcare professionals. A hardware and software integrated wearable device will be developed.

projects.title: FPGA implementation of chaotic cryptographic algorithms for secure communication
projects.description: Live GPS tracking of drones using satellite data Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), such as GPS, enable real-time positioning and navigation for a wide range of civilian applications, including unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs or drones). The most widely used civilian signal is the L1 band (1575.42 MHz), which provides positioning information through satellite broadcasts. Live GPS tracking for drones using civilian L1 band signals relies on the fundamental principle of satellite-based positioning, where signals transmitted by navigation satellites are received by a GNSS module onboard the drone. The L1 band, operating at 1575.42 MHz, provides timing and positional data that the receiver processes to determine the drone’s real-time location through trilateration. Since these signals are broadcast in a one-way manner, the drone itself computes its position rather than being actively tracked by satellites. To enable live tracking, this computed position must be transmitted to a ground station or remote system using an additional communication link such as radio telemetry or cellular networks. While it is technically possible to capture and process raw L1 signals using software-defined radio, practical implementations typically use dedicated GNSS modules for efficiency and reliability. Overall, the system combines satellite signal reception with onboard computation and external data transmission to achieve continuous real-time tracking of drones in civilian applications Design of a Microwave This project focuses on the design and development of a compact, efficient Antenna for Targeted Energy microwave antenna capable of delivering targeted energy within the ISM band. Delivery Emphasis will be placed on optimizing antenna performance parameters such as impedance matching, gain, and radiation behaviour, while also analyzing how electromagnetic waves interact with different materials

projects.title: Live GPS tracking of drones using satellite data
projects.description: Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), such as GPS, enable real-time positioning and navigation for a wide range of civilian applications, including unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs or drones). The most widely used civilian signal is the L1 band (1575.42 MHz), which provides positioning information through satellite broadcasts. Live GPS tracking for drones using civilian L1 band signals relies on the fundamental principle of satellite-based positioning, where signals transmitted by navigation satellites are received by a GNSS module onboard the drone. The L1 band, operating at 1575.42 MHz, provides timing and positional data that the receiver processes to determine the drone’s real-time location through trilateration. Since these signals are broadcast in a one-way manner, the drone itself computes its position rather than being actively tracked by satellites. While it is technically possible to capture and process raw L1 signals using software-defined radio, practical implementations typically use dedicated GNSS modules for efficiency and reliability. projects.title: A Unified, Transferable Robotic Control Board for Heterogeneous Robotic Platforms - Team G-Force
projects.description: The purpose of this project (Universal Board for multiple robot) is to eliminate the redundant cycle of designing new electronics for every robot by creating a universal, recoverable control system. It aims to reduce electronic waste and development time by decoupling high-value processing hardware from the mechanical frame. Instead of building from scratch, developers use a single "brain board" that can be swapped between multiple robots—such as rovers, drones, or hexapods—allowing them to skip repetitive low-level wiring and focus on AI-driven mission objectives. The system works through a modular docking mechanism where the main control board, featuring a Teensy 4.1 and Raspberry Pi 5, slides into a robot’s specific interface using pogo-pin connectors. Upon docking, the system reads a unique Hardware ID from the robot. This triggers the Raspberry Pi to automatically upload the correct motor logic and configuration settings to the Teensy. This "plug-and-play" architecture ensures that the electronics are never permanently fixed to a single machine, allowing for rapid redeployment across diverse robotic platforms.

projects.title: Development of a Hybrid Stator Motor Integrated with Triboelectric Nanogenerator (TENG)-Based Wind Energy Harvesting System
projects.description: This proposal outlines the design and development of a hybrid energy system that combines a stator-motor-based triboelectric nanogenerator module with a wind energy-harvesting module. The goal is to make energy use more efficient by using wind energy from the environment. This reduces the need for external power. The TENG module converts mechanical energy from windinduced vibrations or rotations into electrical energy via contact electrification and electrostatic induction. The energy can be stored or used right away to power electronic devices. The proposed mechanism emphasises sustainable materials, compact integration, and enhanced reliability for small-scale applications, including automotive systems and remote devices. The system encourages the use of renewable energy and helps to create self-powered and energy-efficient electromechanical systems by combining conventional motor technology with advanced energy harvesting Jayant Kolte 9960338240

projects.title: VR Based Surgical Simulation for Gesture Rehearsal
projects.description: This project aims to develop a simple prototype of an intra-operative surgical Vivek Jain simulation system. The objective is to create a basic virtual environment where a user can visualize a 3D organ model and interact with it before performing a surgical action. Pre-recorded medical images (CT/MRI) will be used to generate a 3D model, which will be displayed using open-source software such as 3D Slicer or Unity. A basic interaction mechanism (VR/mouse or haptic device) will allow users to simulate actions like pushing or cutting. The system will demonstrate how real-time simulation can assist in planning surgical gestures safely. The project includes model generation, visualization, and simple interaction. This activity will help students understand the importance of simulation, image guidance, and safe surgical planning in modern healthcare. 9416156809

projects.title: Automation of satellite orbit manoeuvre countering the perturbation in Low Earth Orbits
projects.description: Space weather variation and space debris are increasingly becoming a threat to the smooth sailing of satellites. Manual satellite manoeuvre from ground-based control centres is a tedious and sometimes not a fruitful way of doing the process. Automation of such processes is the need of the hour. Towards this end, we would like to propose a detailed modelling of orbit perturbation for LEO satellites due to SRP using Aditya L1 data. Next, we shall develop a semi-analytical algorithm for orbit manoeuvre calculation to counter satellite disintegration. The developed algorithm will be tested for compatibility with satellites’ on-board processors.

projects.title: Design and Fabrication of a digital DIY Machine Kit for Tensile Testing of 3D printed polymeric Dog-bone samples
projects.co_mentor_name: Dr Ravinder Kumar Duvedi, Dr Bikramjit Sharma
projects.description: This learning project focuses on the design and fabrication of a portable bending Dr test machine integrated with a load cell and Arduino-based data acquisition Ravinder system to evaluate the elastic modulus of 3D printing dog-bone shaped Kumar specimens . Students will engage in the complete engineering cycle, including Duvedi conceptual design, mechanical fabrication, sensor integration, calibration, programming, and experimental validation. The system will measure load–deflection behavior during tensile tests and compute elastic modulus through real-time data processing. The project promotes hands-on learning in mechanics of materials, instrumentation, embedded systems, and digital manufacturing. Design and Fabrication of a digital DIY Machine Kit for Fatigue Testing of Polymer samples. This learning project focuses on the design and fabrication of a portable cyclic bending test machine integrated with a load cell and Arduino-based data acquisition system to evaluate the fatigue testing of polymer samples. Students will engage in the complete engineering cycle, including conceptual design, mechanical fabrication, sensor integration, calibration, programming, and experimental validation. The system will measure load–deflection behavior during fatiguestrength through real-time data processing. The project promotes hands-on learning in mechanics of materials, instrumentation, embedded systems, and digital manufacturing.
